title: Aliro, zerothird, and Cisco Demonstrate Operational Entanglement-Based QKD on Enterprise Routers - Quantum Computing Report
gist: Aliro Technologies and Austrian scale-up zerothird demonstrated with Cisco at its Vimercate, Italy Photonics Center a live entanglement-based QKD (BBM92 protocol) network with automated orchestration feeding keys directly into production Cisco 8000 Series routers via the Cisco SKIP interface for quantum-safe MACsec sessions.

themes.theme: Entanglement-based QKD moving from lab to production networking hardware

insights.claim: Aliro Technologies, zerothird, and Cisco demonstrated a live entanglement-based quantum key distribution (eQKD) network running the BBM92 protocol directly on production-grade Cisco 8000 Series routers at the Cisco Photonics Center in Vimercate, Italy, rather than isolated research-grade hardware.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Moving eQKD from research-grade to commercial off-the-shelf routing hardware is a concrete commercialization milestone that shortens the path to enterprise quantum-safe networking deployment.
insights.quote: This demonstration transitions the technology into standard enterprise infrastructure by combining Aliro Orchestrator software with zerothird’s automated key-generation hardware.

insights.claim: Generated quantum keys are fed directly into Cisco 8000 Series routers via the Cisco SKIP interface, enabling live quantum-safe MACsec encrypted sessions without requiring custom application refactoring at the datacenter.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A standardized interface (Cisco SKIP) for injecting quantum keys into existing MACsec workflows lowers integration friction, which is often the biggest adoption barrier for quantum-safe networking in enterprise IT.
insights.quote: the generated quantum keys are fed directly into Cisco 8000 Series routers via the Cisco SKIP interface. This architectural hook enables the routers to establish live, quantum-safe MACsec encrypted sessions.
insights.locator: Seamless Integration with Production Datacenter Infrastructure

insights.claim: The system's Aliro Orchestrator continuously monitors quantum bit error rates (QBER), polarization drift, and photon detection statistics, and automatically triggers self-healing workflows (recalibration, traffic rerouting, or link termination) when metrics degrade beyond thresholds.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Automated self-healing and eavesdropper detection at the software orchestration layer is a maturity signal that eQKD networks can be operated as standard IT services rather than requiring specialized quantum physicists on staff.
insights.quote: If operational metrics degrade or exceed configured thresholds, the Orchestrator’s alerts engine triggers automated self-healing workflows, such as on-the-fly recalibration, traffic rerouting, or secure link termination.
